reviews.review_text:
Announcing Deadzone: Rogue 2 while the original game is still relatively fresh out of Early Access, missing content, and plagued by a number of issues is certainly a choice.
The game itself is good and has a lot of potential, but seeing the developers already shift their focus to a sequel makes it feel less like long-term support and more like a quick cash grab. There have also been previous concerns about unfinished and abandoned projects, so I have little reason to believe this situation will be any different.
If you buy Deadzone: Rogue expecting major bugs to be fixed or new features and progression systems to be added, you will most likely be disappointed.
Doesnt finish first game, starts charting for second.  Nahhhh.

Look guys, I really like Deadzone Rogue, it's a great roguelite shooter that feels really good to play. 

But can I politely ask what the ♥♥♥♥ you're doing? 

Your game is not even a year old, and you're already shifting all attention to a sequel without making attempts to continue expanding on a game you've already got. You guys show you can make a good functional shooter so why are you trying to pull an EA Sports and pump-dump as many of these games as possible? It's like you don't even want to attempt building up a game as much as possible to get the most potential out of it, you just want a money conveyor belt.

Everything you're claiming you need Deadzone Rogue 2 to do can just be an expansion. Your claim that it would "fracture" the player base to do so is also nonsensical because you're doing that *anyway* by abandoning a game that people like to work on another one. You're acting like a deadbeat dad off to get milk; you're supposed to be a company that supports what it puts out. I say again, NOTHING you are claiming about Deadzone Rogue 2 justifies it being a sequel that couldn't be used to expand upon and add more to Deadzone Rogue. We already have a Garten of Banban slop factory; we don't need a second.

You guys seriously need a wake up call to pull your heads out of your butts and stop sniffing your own farts. All you're gonna do is push even more people away to games that'll actually get proper support, love and care.

So on that note, everybody go wishlist Deep Rock Galactic: Rogue Core instead. Because gamers exist out of spite. 

Now go sort your bloody priorities out, Prophecy Games.

im kind of confused as to why / how you're making a sequel for a game with so much that isn't fixed in the first one / more content to be added. Not to mention the trailer for the second game looks like DLC. Kind of an Israeli move imo

This is a great game, and is highly worth $25.    Notice that the people complaining about the game all have 90 hours of playtime, but are complaining that a small game company isn't making more content for a $25 game, and is instead making part 2 of this game.   Apparently small game companies should work for free and support a $25 dollar game with content for 20 years.  

That's not a bad deal, 50-100 hours of entertaining roguelite gameplay for $25.  

Ignore the buffoonery and purchase the game, you won't be sorry.

Had some potential but never really grew in depth and breadth throughout its hype in early access. Developers were known for dumping their games after the initial buy-in, and their announcement of a second game barely six months after the release of the first proves that they are still doing it.

Truly hours wasted.

Could have been solid with a bit more work but less than a year after release the devs are already abandoning it for a sequel that will be equally unfinished. Save your money and don't support a studio that can't finish what they start.

*Recommended with caveats*
I have had so much positive to say about this game, and have supported it as it developed. That said, it left early access last August and clearly still needs some love. Announcing a sequel this soon for a game which spent 2 years or more (I can't even remember any more) in early access, after I waited months for them to fix the controller support they BROKE (IT ALREADY WORKED), when there is plenty that could be done here, it just doesn't sit well. I will recommend this game, because I got my money's worth. I will not recommend buying the sequel because they haven't finished this one, and making another is a slap in the face to those of us who bought this in early access. That's just a cash grab using what they learned in the last 3-4 years. When the new one comes out I'll be one of the many who (rightfully) downvote it, and I doubt I'll buy it unless they do enough with DZ:R between then and now to change my mind.
Already working on a 2nd game when the first is barely out of EA is a massive red flag. the game itself is decent however it needs work. We shall see if it gets it.

Annouced a second game really fast after pushing this one out. Which this game cme from their first game which they closed down just before putting this one into early access. I will never trust these Devs or Publishers again. They would rather pump and dump their games than put the time into making them better.

Enjoyed what I played of this game but announcing a sequel to a very new game is disgusting. Instead of incorporating new ideas into this game they are creating a separate game for a blatant cash grab, scumbags, don't support this developer team

yea this was a great game. but im with everyone else. rushing a 2nd game instead of just updating this one.. shove that second one up your ass cuz you just cost yourselves everything by doing that. greedy bastards.
The game just released and its already getting a sequel. Cant help but feel this is all half baked for extra money.

Coming out with a sequel in less than a year is obviously a cash grab. Why not make it a DLC at most. Now its going to be another $25 game. I'm glad so many people are review bombing this. This is horrible for a game.

--- BE WARNED ---
The dev stayed true to their (not so positive) roots...

Early discussions on steam for this game, raised the abandonment of previous games, whilst trying to remain positive above continued Dead Zone Rogue content. 

Next an announcement is made for Deadzone 2 when the first one feels and basically is half finished. 

A complete money grab.

If you ignore that... The game is fun and can be challenging, great for co-op. 

But yea, can't recommend it purely based on it being unfinished and buggy with the announcement of the DZ2 shortly after the first is considered as "released"
The direct sequel announcement right after early access makes you wonder why you burned money for the first game.

I enjoyed this game a lot. I hopped more would be added and make it a great game! instead they are ditching it and making a second one already its clearly a money grab just like the dlc for the first game. this should tell you all you need to know about the direction this company wants to go in and I wont be part of it.
One update and now they've decided to work on Deadzone 2 lol. Can probably expect the same tall tales to amount to nothing when the next iteration is released
the game just launched and they already announced a sequel lmfao

Dont get me wrong, great game. but the fact that it isnt even a year old and the developers have already announced a sequel to a yet unfinished game is baffling, the game does not even have an ending... great gameplay by a poor company.
Announcing a second game when 1.0 came out less than a year ago is actually crazy braindead developers
Not a fan of the announcement of the second game before the first game has been fully fleshed out and complete.

Yeah, no. Don't let the play time fool you, I had high hopes for this game, and there is a fun set of bones there that I've spent some enjoyable time with, but only having this in early access for 3 months in the state it was in is insane. Now they've announced a sequel, and frankly this game still feels quite unfinished.

They added several new elements and weapon types, as well as a really fun skill synergy system, but only let you use them in the newest missions, so for 3/4 of the game you're using what feels like half a toolkit. The dialog is wooden, and the "story" feels like a placeholder that never got replaced. Movement needs work, sprint and dash being on one key in a game that plays at this speed is nuts, it feels awful. Tons of little things like that they could have been fixing instead of working on another game. There's potential everywhere, thus the EA purchase, but no delivery.

Now that I have seen footage of the second game, it is blindingly clear that these updates should have been added to this game as a part of the early access period. Instead they rushed out half a product and went for the cash grab. I genuinely can't fathom the thought process here; it's a digital product, you make money when someone buys a copy, it doesn't matter if it's the same game you've been selling or a new one. A killer 1.0 release would have sold gangbusters, people love these kinds of games. You already sell (also half assed, I must note) cosmetic packs so you clearly understand there are ways to monetize a game post release. This one needed a lot more time in the oven, it really sucks to see those updates lumped together into a hasty, unnecessary sequel.

Just to put it in perspective, the time from EA release to 1.0 was 3 months. 9 months later we're already seeing footage for DR2 and being told the demo is coming out soon. At the end of the day, it's hard not to feel like I was robbed of money I thought I was contributing to the continued development of this project, only to watch it funneled into a sequel (that I will now have to pay again for if I want to play the content I already paid to have developed) while this was left unfinished.

I'm beyond disappointed. Technically no promises were broken, I guess, but what a letdown. Can't in good conscience recommend it if this is all it's ever gonna be. Bummer.

Pretty decent and fun game with some Doom and Titanfall elements scattered throughout, however it is full of Ai generated art, writing, and most likely voice acting. The store page does not disclose this, which is incredibly dishonest and misleading. Don't buy into this slop, not even a year after release they're announcing a second game, while the first is still unfinished in some areas.

Sidenote: I do congratulate the developers on their fantastic optimization, and I wish they put that level of effort into making a better game :/

Great game. Definitely recommended. Do not mind the weirdos that buy a completed game for $25, then expect live service treatment and endless free new content for years.

You paid for a product, you got the product. Now they're making a new one. Old one is good. New one will hopefully be even better.

Loved the game, bought the supporter pack. Felt very promising and then a sequel gets announced with nothing different compared to the first game in the trailer. Sad to say the least.

An important note I just learned is that this dev has abandoned previous projects.
I put way more hours into this game than I expected to, and still playing. Can't wait for the second one.
This game does not feel finished and they already announced Deadzone 2.... So I guess they aren't going to add much to this game. Better not buy this.

After being disappointed that they are releasing a whole new game instead of more content to this one I decided to look at their other games. They have another game made in 2024 called "Tribes 3:Rivals" that they abandoned. I wouldn't recommend purchasing anything from this company because it's likely to be abandoned within the year.

Fun game to play coop with friends and worth picking up if it's on sale. That said, announcing a sequel so soon comes across as the devs abandoning the game and making a money grab. It's sad they never spent more time fixing bugs, fleshing out the levels, and polishing the game to make it the gem it could've been.
Game is kinda fun, but still in early access state and now abandoned by developers. Do not buy

1.0 for this game was October 6th. Not even a year after release instead of fixing the original they are cash grabbing at a number 2. This is absolutely unacceptable practice.  This game had a lot of potential but to find out the dev has spent their time making another game instead of supporting and fixing this game is disappointing.

Wild announcing a sequel when this game isn't finished/polished yet at all. We assume this means abandonware and the updates and fixes will be absent from here on for this game even though it was just released from early access.

Didn't even notice the development team was moving on to a squeal, less then a year after release, before even finishing their current game. It's too bad to, this game seemed fun. I was even planning to buy a few copies for my friends so we can play it together. Ah well, another solid start of a game becoming abandon ware. Should of seen this coming, after all I liked the idea behind this one like a few other abandoned games out there.

Edit: Found out why they're doing their own game dirty like this. This is Hi-Rez studios just with a new name. 

If you don't know who Hi-Rez are, that's easy. Hi-Rez is a game developer company that for what ever reason hates their player base. If you don't play their game exactly the way they want you to, they start taking away the content you are playing to try and force the players to play their way. When that fails, as it always does with their toilet roll long list of canceled games, they try re-adding the content they remove and try to charge retail AAA tile prices for each little thing (weapons, class', game modes, etc) or lock them behind a subscription.

I am biased against Hi-Rez mostly because a good chunk of those addandonware games I menched above where made by them. They have a gift for taking a possible gold mine of a game and turning it into lead.
The game is severely undercooked and the devs have the AUDACITY to announce a sequel?! God I wish I could refund this, but I can't.

The game is half finished and they're already announcing Deadzone: Rogue 2.
Don't get me wrong, the game is good but it doesn't feel quite finished yet and the devs seem be working on a follow up instead of completing this one.

If you typically avoid the Roguelike genre (like me) or are just bored with the same old formula you need to play Deadzone: Rogue. While it keeps the addictive "fight, die, upgrade, repeat" loop, it completely flips the script on how you approach an action-roguelike room.  However, it is not a perfect game. After spending dozens of hours grinding out runs, there are some noticeable cracks in the steel hull of the ISS-X.  

PROS: 
- Every single sector begins in total stealth. The game gives you a moment to breathe, scan the layout, and plan an execution strategy before the absolute chaos drops.
- Hitting mechanical weak spots, managing shield recharges, and fighting recoil feels closer to a tight tactical shooter than an arcade bullet hell.
- Combining elements like Void and Shock to trigger screen-clearing chain reactions is intuitive and is very cool to watch.

CONS:
- Higher difficulties are tightly locked behind individual mission completion. Once your permanent progression scales up, you out-power the base difficulties, turning the mid-game into a repetitive loop to unlock the real challenges.
- The movement is usually fast and fluid, but occasionally getting physically stuck on background collision or debris during a high-stakes firefight causes plenty of unfair deaths.

Looking Forward:
Prophecy Games has already announced Deadzone: Rogue 2, which shifts the fight from the cramped space corridors down to a post-apocalyptic Earth ruled by an evil machine intelligence. With a free playable demo arriving soon, I really hope they take the tactical foundation established here and use the sequel to smooth out these progression and geometry flaws.

The Verdict:
Despite some pacing issues and occasional geometric clutter, it remains another indie gem.  It takes the progression of a great roguelite, the gunplay of a high-tier sci-fi shooter, and injects a layer of strategy that the genre desperately needs.  Absolutely worth it!
Was very OK for an indie early access but the devs just teased they're making a new game when this one isn't even finished

Deadzone Rogue is my favorite roguelike game right now. The gameplay loop is addicting and it’s easy to lose hours in it.

But after over a year, the game still has bugs, rough edges, and feels like it needed more content, or even a DLC before moving on to Part 2.

That’s why I’m leaving a Not Recommended review for now. I understand the devs saying the current game has technical limits, but as a player who was expecting more content after the final mission, it feels too early for a sequel.

Prophecy Games is also a smaller studio, so I’m honestly worried most of the focus will shift to Part 2 instead of improving Part 1. (I still face ping issues btw)

Based on their explanation for making a new game instead of a DLC, Part 2 honestly sounds like it could end up being the better and more complete version with all the improvements they’re planning.

Because of that, I only recommend buying Deadzone Rogue 1 on a big discount right now. The game is still really fun, I just feel like it could have had way more content and polish before moving on to a sequel. If you haven’t played it yet, it’s probably better to wait for Part 2, watch reviews, and decide from there.
Game is fun, however feels very unfinished still. Now they announced a 2nd game already without having updated the first one that much, id be cautious with their next release.

Game was fun in Demo, so friends and I bought it on full release. We completed most of the content and unlocks pretty quickly, but would be looking forwards for updates to play more. Game came out in August of last year, and they already announced a sequel instead of expanding on the first one or making it DLC if they really want to charge for it.

Bro's announcing a second game less than 9 months after the release of the first one, with this one being still very underdeveloped and shockingly lacking in content... What a time to be a roguelike FPS fan, man.

[h2] Do Not Support This [/h2]

This game released in August of 2025. Four months later in December of 2025 was the first major update which brought in a new zone and new missions, among many other things. Another three months later in March of 2026, two new missions were added. So two big updates since release all while throwing out performance fixes and patches in between.

It is now May of 2026, only 9 months since the game released and the developers have just announced a sequel with a trailer saying "the roguelike it back!" It truly never went anywhere. The game still feels unfinished and many people (myself included) were expecting more major updates and content to come. Sequels happen all the time but this is so soon that it feels like a part 2. Only, parts tend to be made with intention and are usually communicated. This feels more like half baked game and a rushed cash grab sequel. 

They fooled me and got my money. Don't be like me. Don't be fooled. Do not support this.

The devs just announced a sequel to this game despite it only being out for 6 months and needing a mountain of work to be truly playable. I was going to let them game grow a little before reviewing it, but I have no faith in this game becoming anything good before the devs abandon it. I honestly didn't even realize the game had left early access when I started playing it.

I beat the first few missions and lost all steam with this game. The permanent upgrades feel meaningless, perks/traits/augments/whatever all feel horribly unbalanced. On higher difficulties, you pretty much need to pick certain ones otherwise you'll get wiped. 

Given that the voices in this game were made by AI, I wouldn't be surprised if half the systems in the game were too.

Combat is so painfully boring. A game can compensate for being generic in one area by being really cool in another, but this game is generic in every area. The guns are boring. The enemies are boring. The environments are boring. The upgrades are boring. There is not an ounce of soul in this entire game. If you want a fun roguelite, go play Roboquest, Hades, or Risk of Rain 2. This game is bad now and will probably be bad in the future.

Announcing a sequel before the first game even hits it's 1 year anniversary is insane. Games aren't movies. You have to stick around and support them for a bit.

In the "Why Not DLC" post the Devs made they mention the million copies sold multiple times. It reeks of a cash grab. 

The game itself is okay. It's not super special and I was hoping that would change with updates. Now that they've shifted to developing a sequel, this game is doomed to be half cooked.

I really enjoy the game and am looking forward to the next one I am also really glad they had a demo because that helped me know I wanted the game before I got it.

its a roguelike shooter and takes a while to level everything, but it gets you into how the games works over the levels. I really enjoyed it. The story is interesting enough and the weapons are unique and fun.

I would like them to update it so you unlock all the hard modes if you beat one at that level instead of having to do each one.

Fun game to play with friends for a while but cannot recommend it with how little content the game has which most likely won't get much better with a sequel being announced despite the first game not being close to a finished product. 

Meta-progression is a grindfest and there isn't that much variation between runs. 

As I mentioned earlier a sequel has been announced which just stinks of a poorly thought out cashgrab. To begin with the game isn't worth 25€ so if you decide to buy it do so when it's on at least 50% sale.

This game was actually pretty great but as of today the second game just got announced with a beta playtest releasing soon. THE FIRST GAME JUST CAME OUT! How are you gonna have people buy your first game, not finish it and leave all the current issues, and then make an announcement that the second game is already releasing soon?! This company is obviously just money grabbing by releasing a second game with another couple levels and a new enemy type that they could definitely just add in a dlc for the first game. Guess the 3rd game will be on the horizon for late 2026 lmao. If this is on a mega sale it might be worth it but this is just a really ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up thing to do to the people who actually bought and play the first game. Guess there wont be any more updates either.

Game has all the foundations of an interesting roguelite with some cool visuals, needs more weapons and mobility options. Shame it will never happen because the devs already announced a sequel, no more updates for this game outside of bug fixes. what a joke
already made a sequel for a game thats been out for less than a full year with 1 (ONE) content update.

Spent all the time this game was in early access working on a sequel instead of finishing this one. Very, very clearly cares more about money than quality of their work. Do NOT support this dev's behavior.

This game needs updated rather than having a sequel. Absolute tragedy that they are just money hungry to make a sequel when their game has not been out for a year. Do not buy this game as it will be useless when the 2nd comes out.

Prophecy Games is just a Hi-Rez subsidiary masquerading as an indie studio to dodge the toxic reputation they've rightfully earned. They carry on the exact same shoddy legacy: hop on whatever trend is hot, release a half baked and/or low effort game, and then completely abandon it. I have no idea how these grifters are still in business.

The game itself is a generic, low variety slog with zero replay value. Upgrades are just boring stat bumps, and the weapons offer next to no actual customization outside of swapping a basic elemental effect. With a measly pool of about ten enemies and the most uninspired procedurally generated rooms I've ever seen, the level design turns the loop into a total chore. You are literally just walking through the same lifeless boxes over and over again.

And instead of actually giving the game any meaningful updates, they just announced Deadzone Rogue 2. It looks exactly the same. They are barely even done abandoning the first one and they're already trying to sell us the exact same asset-flipped loop under a new title. Absolute joke.

I genuinely have no idea how this game's reviews are as positive as they are.

Such a shame, the game was okay. However it was supposed to be a work in progress an early access game. Now you are telling me you are opening a sequel when you haven't even finished the first?!

Yeah no, this game should not be bought under any circumstances. I would not trust this developer.

Is this game bad? No. This game is good, if a little undercooked

Then they announced a sequal...

..I can't in good faith give this game a negative review, because the sequal is independent of this game. If the sequal causes this game to die off.. well that's not THIS game's fault, is it?
But a sequal less than a year after the first game? Guess the company never learns

There are quite a few issues and bugs and glitches that have remained unaddressed by the developers despite this being a full release, and shifting development towards a sequel leads me to believe that they will remain unaddressed for quite a very long time. Avoid this publisher and developer if you do not want to waste money.
The game just turned 9 months old and these devs have decided to do a sequel. At least give this game like a year or two damn

Game is fun but missing a lot of features other games have. The problem is the dev has announced no more content for the game for the game they just released. Instead, wants you to buy a completely new game. The game still has bugs and content is missing in the 3rd and 4th zones compared to 1 and 2. Scummy.

It is INSANE that they would announce Deadzone Rogue 2 while the first game is still WOEFULLY incomplete and unpolished. Dont get me wrong, its a lot of fun and I really had hoped they would flesh this out more out of early access but this is scummy...

Got this on sale for 15$ for that money yeah, it's worth it to play with some friends it's some nice fun.. the thing is why the hell are you guys releasing a 2nd game when this game barely got any updates?? It literally got 1 update with 2 new missions I think? like what are you guys thinking..
Bascially Abandoning the first game (Still missing features.) and asset flipping to a second

I had a lot of fun playing this game. I like rogue-likes, but I'm not a huge FPS player. I come back to this game for a couple hours at a time, and it has not been disappointing. The gameplay is fun and smooth, even on my 3060ti.
The mid-game was a bit of a grind, but once you upgrade all your different stats it gets much easier to have long satisfying runs. 
Lots of different enemies with quite a bit of variety in the mechanics you have to deal with. I wouldn't say there is something revolutionary about any of them, but they are fun to discover, and there was a few times that I had to completely rethink how I was doing things. 
The story is good. Again, it may not be the most original idea you've ever seen in space, but it's fun. You don't have to engage with the story too much if you don't want to, though. That fact is something I appreciate from the devs. I found it much more enjoyable to stumble across things that had some significance to the story instead of sitting through drawn out cutscenes. The world building is done in a way that you can sort of tailor how much you engage with it. That is pretty impressive for a game that takes place entirely in a ship that is essentially a straight line. I don't want to spoil anything from the end of the game, but I found the revelations you get after beating the third sector to be well worth the trouble of getting there.
If you just want to beat the game and see all it has to offer, get the "Melee kills turn you invisible" perk. It makes it absolutely free (maybe rework that perk, devs). If you want to actually feel the difficulty, don't do that.
Definitely recommend this game

PROPHECY GAMES' DECISION TO ABANDON DEAD ZONE ROGUE ONE IS A BETRAYAL!

The community has spoken, and Prophecy Games has chosen to ignore their pleas. Despite the fact that Dead Zone Rogue One remains unfinished and unfulfilled, they're instead opting to move on to a sequel, leaving the original game's fans high and dry.

This is not just a disappointment; it's a slap in the face. The community has been vocal about their desire to see the first game completed, and Prophecy Games has chosen to disregard those voices entirely. Their decision is a clear indication that they're more interested in profiting from a new title than in delivering on the promises they made with Dead Zone Rogue One.

By abandoning this game, Prophecy Games is showing that they have no respect for their fans or the gaming community as a whole. They're willing to sacrifice the trust and goodwill of their audience just to push out a new product and make a quick buck.

DO NOT SUPPORT PROPHECY GAMES' DECISION TO ABANDON DEAD ZONE ROGUE ONE. DEMAND BETTER FROM YOUR GAME DEVELOPERS.

Boy, was I wrong for trusting you after Starsiege: Deadzone. For anyone who doesn't know, Starsiege: Deadzone was the game before Deadzone: Rogue, with a different approach - a battle royale extraction-shooter style game. Suddenly, during early access, they chose to shut down all the servers and go completely radio silent, without refunding any money people had spent on the game (even though the game itself was free). The game was genuinely fun, and people like myself enjoyed the fact that it felt more unique compared to other games of the same genre.

Deadzone: Rogue basically reuses most of the weapons, themes, and overall ideas from the old game, just with more arcade-style implementations to fit the roguelite genre.

When I first saw this game releasing in early access and noticed it was made by the same developers, I didn’t want to spend a single penny because I was afraid history would repeat itself. After seeing you guys actually working on it for some time (even if AI was a big contributor), I decided to give you one more chance, and honestly, it was worth it. The game is fun and enjoyable, even with some bugs here and there. There are a LOT of improvements you could still make and plenty of ways to expand the game by adding new zones, weapons, perks, and mechanics.

Then, all of a sudden, you announce Deadzone: Rogue 2, which was a huge disappointment. Trying to justify it by saying “we’re limited with this game because we're afraid that experiments will make people not like the game” is honestly funny to me. You could literally continue introducing new content the same way you’ve done until now:  by adding new zones, enemies, perks, abilities, and concepts, then fine-tuning them over time. Maybe some players wouldn’t like a new zone, but then add custom lobbies where players can choose which zones they want included. There are so many different approaches you could take, yet you chose to make another game instead, most likely for more money. Shame.

I personally wanted to keep enjoying this game, but it seems greed and money are more important than building a loyal community around a genuinely good game you created. I honestly wouldn’t recommend anyone buy games from these developers, because it feels like they’re just going to repeat the same cycle all over again.

You can race through the base content in a few hours besides maybe one or two maps. Which is normally fine, but announcing a second game? Eight months after the first releases? Don't get me wrong, I don't mind sequels, but you're basically just making a game with content that should have been added to this one. This is the next step past day one DLC. Don't get this.

i played this game loved it and reccomended it to so many people but to screw everyone over like this and announce a second game while the first has so little content and promised more is frankly disgusting.

This game was released less than a year ago and you're asking people to buy another one instead of updating the current one.

Such a shame considering the game is fun and in a solid state, but instead of updating you're going to greed and make people pay full price for a new game.

Absolute scam.
Cash grab DEVS, didn't finish the 1st game and announce a 2nd.  Uninstall and DONT SUPPORT THESE GUYS.

Released Aug 11 2025. Sequel announced May 2026 lmao. So not even a year long lifecycle for this game? I mean I guess Prophecy Games has been known for doing this in the past so I'm not that surprised. Just disappointed

The good ol let's take it out of early access and call the game finished. My son is older than this game and I don't have another kid yet. But this guy has an incubation period for 9 months and says welp time for a sequel. I don't usually write steam reviews but don't buy this game or the sequel. I was waiting for the game to cook some more before I actually finished it, but since the game is getting a sequel looks like I won't be.
RELEASE DATE: Aug 11, 2025 btw.

This game has such amazing potential. Been playing since early access and loving it. Game was coming along so well then right when the devs hit their groove with good updates and fixes they've abandend it early and announced a sequel. Game just came out of early access and still isn't finished. 

What had the potential to be a 10/10 game is now left incomplete. What a dissapointment.

The first game isn't even completed yet they already plan to make a sequel. I have no problem with having a sequel to a good game, however, the first one isn't polished even though it has been very fun to play.
pushing a 2nd game that looks like a dlc for the 1st game is greedy bullsh!t. this game is only a few months out of early access. will never buy a game from this developer ever again!

review bombing for the news of recent "2nd game" post they just had. 

and i speak for a metric ton of people when I say- "What the ♥♥♥♥".

this game also doesn't need to be 25 smackaroons if ya'll are just gonna leave it unfinished.

Game shows great promise but seriously lacks content and has too many bugs to be out of early access. In no way is the game in a state to have a sequel announced and only 9 months after this game "fully" released.

Very good and fun game, I WOULD recommend it, if not for the obvious cash grab mentality.

This game still feels incomplete and is riddled with bugs, but they're already working on a sequel and are so far in development that they will release a demo for Deadzone rogue 2 soon...
Decent game, quite fun both solo and co-op. However abandoning a half finished game for a sequel, screams cash grab scam.

Game barely released 3 months ago and they already announced a sequel instead of expanding upon and fixing/polishing this one which is very much content deprived and still has many game breaking bugs. Having a demo soon for the sequel implies they've been working on it before they even fully released this game. What a slap in the face of the early access supporters for this game.

TLDR - The game is fun, but unfinished, and it has already been abandoned by the devs just months after 'release'. 

This is a pretty fun FPS roguelike, with good shooting and an interesting setting/ enemy designs. 
The game does feel like its still in early access, with some balance definitely needed and the story un finished, yet the devs have already decided to announce a sequel just 9 months after the release of this game. 

The upcoming sequel should just be an expansion for this game, either as DLC or even better as bonus content, considering the story is incomplete. 

It leaves a really sour taste to abandon this game so quickly in a desperate attempt to get more money from people. I wont be buying the sequel at any price, despite how much I enjoy the bare bones of this game.  Very disappointing to see and if I knew the devs would do this, I never would have purchased this game.

I knew I was wrong in giving my money to this HiRez rebrand after what happened with their employees and playerbase. When this game came out, people asked "Is this also a scam". Now, not even a year later, they're releasing not a content update, but a 2nd separate game you can buy! 

This game has the good hi-rez movements that they have down, but it's VERY lacking in content.

Announcing a sequel not even a full year after the first game is released is the most obvious cash grab that I have ever seen. 

This game is no where near complete enough for that

The game is pretty good but it only released in August 2025 and the devs have already opted to make a second one instead of updates or even dlc. It's pretty clearly a money grab based off unexpected success and I can't support that

Was fun for a bit.

Trying to money grab for a second game less than a year after first game's launch is insane.
Make it DLC.

When I played the demo for THIS game, Deadzone: Rogue 1, I found it to be a ton of fun. both my friend and I had a boat load of fun. The game was challenging, very challenging at first, but as you learn and get some permanent upgrades you start to make headway and for us twin mini guns killed the final boss. We were hooked and when this game dropped we were on it. 

We immediately were a bit disappointed. The game had been brought down from a customer rated 10 to a critique rated 10. The game was easy we BREEZED through the first area and at the start of the EA that was kind of it, there was a little more, but none of it captured that exact feel that hooked us from the demo. We figured they'd add harder difficulties and what not. Dropped the game for like a year and figured it'd cook.

I look at my feed today, "Deadzone Rogue 2 demo coming soon". Oh cool I guess I must have missed the 2 or 3 major updated needed to get the game moving. No doubt the rest of the community was so enamored with it they gave relentless feedback and now we have that gold seal 9/10 experience here. What's that? Recent reviews mixed? The game isn't finished and you out here telling me that you have a sequel that looks exactly like THIS game with some new mobs that should be added to fill this game out instead of a sequel. Even if the devs said, "Don't worry we hired a brand new team with all your money so we can work on both at the same time" Bro I don't buy and start reading the second book when I'm half way through the first. I don't watch ep 2 of a new anime before ep 1. I even muted the trailer cause I don't want spoilers. It just sucks. A demo that had my faith in games coming back immediately drops the ball. 

It's unfortunate to see corporate greed kill a series I would have happily dropped thirty dollars on DLC over a year and then bought a second game in like 2 or 3. Now I'm not wanting to touch anything that has this companies stamp on it.

This developer is incapable of finishing a game.

We both know you have a backlog of games that you haven't started or meant to get back to someday. 
Go do that instead of this.

Kinda weird to be announcing a sequel when the game is still needing fixes, has multiple  bugs, and generally still feels like an early access game, the game content is not that massive, and could use more variation of skins, enemies, level areas, or all of the above.

Are you serious? The game hasn't been out very long and not even a year ago released out of early access, 2 cosmetic packs that barely add anything and don't even look much better than the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ for your guns. Only reason to get them is to support the devs or a suit skin really, the devs mind you that I don't think should be supported because they're just pulling support to make a sequel. Yes, they're gonna still do pathces/bug fixes but not new content?

This dev team has a history of doing things like this, just pumping out slop and then abandoning it shortly after. I thought this game was decently fun but felt that it really needed more content and continuous updates for years with the 25 dollar price point, It gets tedious real fast. I kept playing bc I can't find many games with a similar gameplay style and I was excited for the synergies, yet I was very disappointed with the quality of the synergies. I was hoping for some chaotic awesome combos. Based on the trailer for Deadzone Rogue 2, it looks like it would just be a new update for this one. I highly recommend the devs to cancel the 2nd game and instead continue support for this game. It's not too late to turn things around and fix this.
maybe fix the first game before trying to scam us for another empty, repetitive rouge*lite*. it hasnt even been a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ year since this piece of ♥♥♥♥ came out btw.

It's absolutely not worth it for 25€, especially since they just announced a sequel. Mind you not they released into early access last year just before may, then it was in early access for about 3 months an was vomited out of it.

So I was waiting for some more updates to jump in and give this game the try I promised it.

Then I look over and see the announcement for Deadzone Rogue 2... I literally had to go check I wasn't crazy. It's been less than a year since this dropped. Seems like a solid pump/dump to me. -sigh-

Not too happy with the announcement of number 2. It's a good game but it didn't feel finished EA when it launched and now we see why: they saved all the content for a second game. No one should be trusting this developer. Who wants to take bets on #2 being a live service game with battle passes?
